notes: R8 keeps the same endpoint fixed and only raises retrieval pressure on a bounded heavier-family suite drawn from the preserved direct baseline.
The harder suite is limited to one 10x retrieval-horizon row from each of the top four direct-baseline families.
Linear-vs-Hull decode parity is kept explicit only on a bounded uniform-load probe of the top two heaviest admitted harder rows so the gate remains bounded under unattended execution.
Pressure is exported quantitatively as event, read/write, candidate-depth, stack-depth, and control-transfer growth relative to the admitted R6 8x rows.

supported_here: R8 keeps the same direct-baseline endpoint fixed and raises retrieval pressure only on a bounded heavier-family suite.
Admitted rows export explicit event, read/write, candidate-depth, stack-depth, and control-transfer pressure metrics instead of implying pressure qualitatively.
unsupported_here: R8 does not widen semantics, reopen arbitrary compiled-language claims, or justify a broader systems-superiority statement.
Any contradiction-only route to E1c remains narrower than a general endpoint expansion.
